claim: A delegated sub-operator shall receive its OWN crystal, a CLAUDE.md generated from its sub-domain.
why: The Delegation.returns conclusions-only contract (R-delegation-conclusions-only) made concrete for sub-operators. BUILD-TRIGGER status: the historical spawn_agent tool (composing per-agent CLAUDE.md into the subagent prompt before dispatch) fired in the Python original on P22.C. Not yet ported in the Go port: spawn-agent is Declared (not ported) in methodology.Tools (internal/methodology/tools_data.go), and the Go generator (internal/generator) produces a single consolidated root CLAUDE.md with no per-agent sub-operator crystal files -- because with one OP-director and zero active sub-agents there is no second agent to hand a crystal to (R-claude-md-consolidates-when-single-agent). RE-ATOMIZED Wave 8 move 2 (2026-07-03): the prior compound claim ('shall receive its OWN crystal AND return CONCLUSIONS only') had its second half trimmed because it duplicated R-delegation-conclusions-only verbatim; this atom now owns only the crystal-provisioning half. The substance remains live: when a real sub-operator is delegated, it must boot from its own substrate-derived CLAUDE.md rather than raw context; the materializer for that crystal is unported substrate that arrives alongside R-sub-agent-crystal-triad's per-agent generation. PROSE (not ENFORCED): no enforcer exists in the Go port.
